entries.statement: Ability to identify and communicate when analytical findings challenge prevailing business narratives, presenting disconfirming evidence constructively and with appropriate context.
Ability to select the appropriate analytical method for a given business question, balancing statistical rigor, time constraints, data availability, and audience comprehension capacity.
Ability to distinguish between correlation and causation in analytical findings, communicating the distinction and its implications for decision-making clearly to business audiences.

Ability to exercise structured judgment in data-informed decision-making, weighing quantitative evidence against qualitative context, assessing relative costs and benefits of analytical approaches, and recommending actions when data is incomplete or ambiguous.
entries.strm_gap_source: STRM-001-ONET: O*NET 2.B.4.e (Judgment and Decision Making) — STS 0.406 to AB-A-002
